title_en: Conditions of the Narrator: Justice and Precision
content_en:
The core of Al-Kifayah fi Ilm al-Riwayah is its systematic treatment of the qualifications required of a hadith narrator. Al-Khatib al-Baghdadi identifies two fundamental conditions that every narrator in an acceptable chain of transmission must meet: adalah (moral uprightness or justice) and dabt (precision or accuracy of retention). These two requirements structure the entire evaluative apparatus of hadith scholarship.

Adalah: Moral Uprightness

Adalah refers to the narrator's moral character as a Muslim. Al-Khatib defines it as the condition of a Muslim who is legally mature (baligh), mentally competent (aqil), free from major sins (kaba'ir), and not habitually committing minor sins. A narrator who is a known liar, who publicly commits shameful acts, or who holds heretical theological positions that lead to deliberate distortion of reports is disqualified from consideration.

Al-Khatib addresses a key jurisprudential question: how do we know that a narrator possesses adalah? He distinguishes between narrators whose uprightness is established by explicit testimony from trusted scholars (tawthiq sarih) and those whose uprightness is presumed because they were accepted and cited by earlier reliable scholars without known criticism. He also discusses the threshold at which scholarly criticism of a narrator becomes significant enough to reject his narrations.

An important subsection addresses whether the narrator's profession, social class, or sectarian affiliation affects the assessment of his adalah. Al-Khatib's nuanced treatment acknowledges that narrators from various theological camps — Mutazilites, Murjites, and even some Shia — have been accepted by hadith masters when their accuracy of transmission was demonstrably sound and their distortions confined to their own theological views rather than embedded in their reports about the Prophet.

Dabt: Precision of Retention

Dabt refers to the narrator's technical competence in receiving, retaining, and transmitting reports accurately. Al-Khatib subdivides this into dabt al-sadr (precision of the chest, i.e., memorization) and dabt al-kitab (precision of the book, i.e., reliable written records). A narrator with an excellent memory who transmits from memory is evaluated by the standard of dabt al-sadr; one who transmits from his written notes is evaluated by whether those notes were accurately made and safely preserved.

Al-Khatib examines the signs of precision and its absence. A narrator who contradicts other reliable narrators, whose narrations contain unusual features not found elsewhere, who made many errors when tested, or whose reports were frequently rejected by those who heard them — all these are signs of weak dabt. He provides extensive examples from the biographical literature, naming narrators whose reliability was degraded due to old age, illness, loss of their written records, or simple carelessness in transmission.

The Interaction of Adalah and Dabt

Al-Khatib concludes this section with a discussion of how the two conditions interact. A narrator may be morally impeccable but technically imprecise, or technically precise but morally compromised. In both cases, scholars have distinguished the circumstances: a slightly imprecise narrator whose narrations are confirmed by others is acceptable; a morally compromised narrator whose reports happen to be accurate still raises concerns because his motivation to transmit faithfully is questionable. The ideal narrator — the fully trustworthy (thiqah) — combines both qualities at the highest level, and such narrators form the backbone of the Sahih collections of al-Bukhari and Muslim.
content_id:
Inti dari Al-Kifayah fi Ilm al-Riwayah adalah pembahasannya yang sistematis tentang kualifikasi yang diperlukan dari seorang perawi hadis. Al-Khatib al-Baghdadi mengidentifikasi dua syarat fundamental yang harus dipenuhi oleh setiap perawi dalam sanad yang dapat diterima: (1) al-adalah (keadilan moral), yakni integritas karakter perawi sebagai seorang Muslim yang taat; dan (2) al-dhabt (ketepatan/akurasi), yakni kemampuan perawi untuk mengingat dan menyampaikan hadis secara akurat.

Mengenai syarat keadilan, Al-Khatib menjelaskan bahwa seorang perawi harus memenuhi lima kriteria: Islam, aqil (berakal), baligh (dewasa), menjauhi dosa-dosa besar, dan menghindari perbuatan-perbuatan yang merendahkan martabat (muru'ah). Beliau membahas setiap kriteria ini secara rinci, termasuk pertanyaan-pertanyaan yang diperdebatkan seperti apakah perawi yang melakukan dosa-dosa kecil tertentu masih dapat diterima riwayatnya, dan bagaimana menilai seorang perawi yang telah bertaubat dari kefasikan masa lalunya.

Mengenai syarat ketepatan, Al-Khatib membedakan antara dua jenis: dhabt al-shadr (hafalan yang kuat dalam dada) dan dhabt al-kitab (keakuratan dan pemeliharaan catatan tertulis). Seorang perawi yang memiliki hafalan yang sangat kuat memiliki kedudukan yang lebih tinggi dari perawi yang bergantung pada catatan, meskipun keduanya dapat diterima selama memenuhi standar minimum yang ditetapkan.

Al-Khatib juga membahas secara panjang lebar tentang bagaimana cara mengetahui keadilan seorang perawi — apakah cukup dengan persaksian orang-orang yang terpercaya, ataukah diperlukan penyelidikan yang lebih mendalam. Beliau mengulas berbagai pendapat ulama dan menetapkan metodologi yang kemudian menjadi standar dalam ilmu jarh wa ta'dil.

Pembahasan tentang dua syarat ini merupakan fondasi dari seluruh bangunan ilmu hadis, karena pada akhirnya kesahihan sebuah hadis bergantung pada kualitas manusia-manusia yang membawanya dari generasi ke generasi.
